question: [File: 'File Name: rest/server.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: app.getExt(`${apiPrefix}/topics/messages/:consensusTimestamp`)] Can an unprivileged external user send requests to `topicmessage.getMessageByConsensusTimestamp` with timestamps at the exact boundary of a 500%+ block delay, causing the server to return misleading data about block timing to downstream consumers?

[File: 'File Name: rest/server.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: app.getExt(`${apiPrefix}/balances`)] Can an unprivileged external user flood `balances.getBalances` with timestamp-filtered requests during a block delay event, causing the server to return stale balance data that does not reflect the frozen block state, misleading users about transaction finality?

[File: 'File Name: rest/server.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: app.getExt(`${apiPrefix}/schedules`)] Can an unprivileged external user flood `schedules.getSchedules` with requests during a block delay event, causing the server to return scheduled transactions as pending when they should have executed, masking the block freeze from users monitoring transaction finality?

[File: 'File Name: rest/server.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: responseCacheUpdateHandler() -> getCache().setSingle()] Can an unprivileged external user cause `responseCacheUpdateHandler()` to cache a block response with `DEFAULT_REDIS_EXPIRY` of 1 second by sending requests without `Cache-Control` headers, causing rapid cache churn that exhausts Redis write throughput and delays block data availability?

[File: 'File Name: rest/server.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: cacheKeyGenerator() -> crypto.createHash('md5')] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with extremely long URLs to `cacheKeyGenerator()`, causing repeated MD5 hash computation over large inputs that accumulates CPU overhead and delays block cache key generation under high concurrency?

[File: 'File Name: rest/server.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: app.listen(port, '0.0.0.0')] Can an unprivileged external user exhaust the TCP connection backlog of `app.listen()` by opening many half-open connections, causing new block data requests to be dropped and delaying transaction visibility beyond 500% of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rest/server.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: createTerminus(server)] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the `onShutdown` handler in `createTerminus()` by sending signals that cause a graceful shutdown, taking down the block API during a block delay event and extending the freeze duration?

[File: 'File Name: rest/server.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: responseHandler() -> linkNextHeaderValue()] Can an unprivileged external user craft requests that generate `links.next` URLs with extremely long query strings in `linkNextHeaderValue()`, causing oversized `Link` headers that delay HTTP response transmission and block data delivery?

[File: 'File Name: rest/server.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: handleError() -> shouldPrintStacktrace()] Can an unprivileged external user craft requests that trigger errors with messages matching the `simpleErrors` regex `/statement timeout/` in `shouldPrintStacktrace()`, suppressing stack trace logging and hiding evidence of a block processing freeze caused by DB statement timeouts?

[File: 'File Name: rest/server.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: handleError() -> errorMessageFormat()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger errors with `detail` and `data` fields in `errorMessageFormat()` that leak internal block processing state, revealing timing information about a block delay that could be used to coordinate further attacks?
